Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-30 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two exceptional performers at the top of the momentum rankings, though with distinctly different volatility profiles. VRT ranks #1 with a composite score of 93.1, driven by extraordinary 1-year returns of 281% paired with pristine technical setup (all SMAs above price) and a remarkable 90.9% resilience score on bad SPY days—suggesting it holds up when markets weaken. TTMI follows with even more explosive returns (675% annually), but shows critical stress signals: a much higher bad_spy_score of 2.52 versus VRT's 0.86, indicating it tends to decline sharply during market downturns, and a concerning vol_flag of "thin," suggesting lower liquidity or elevated volatility. Both tickers pass all four SMA gates and show strong consistency in return ratios across timeframes, but VRT's defensive characteristics and stable momentum_signal (-0.12, "clean") present a more balanced risk profile compared to TTMI's fragility during market stress.
